label: Apollo
description: Arcade tools designed for LLMs to interact with Apollo.io sales intelligence

tools.name: EnrichOrganization
tools.description:
Turn a company domain into firmographics (industry, size, revenue, funding,
location) so a rep can qualify and size an account. Consumes one enrichment
credit on a match; when the plan is out of credits the result reports
status=insufficient_credits rather than failing.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Company domain to look up, such as apollo.io. A full URL or www prefix is accepted and normalized.

output.description: The matched company, or found=False when no match.

tools.name: EnrichPerson
tools.description:
Turn a known person into a verified profile with their current role and,
on request, contact details. Provide at least one identifier: an Apollo person
ID, a name (or first and last name), an email, or a LinkedIn URL; an employer
name or domain alone is not enough to identify someone. Consumes one enrichment
credit on a match; when the plan is out of credits the result reports
status=insufficient_credits rather than failing.

parameters.name: person_id
parameters.description: Apollo person ID, such as the id from a people search result. The most reliable identifier when available. Default is None.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Full name of the person. Default is None.

parameters.name: first_name
parameters.description: First name, paired with last_name. Default is None.

parameters.name: last_name
parameters.description: Last name, paired with first_name. Default is None.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Known email address of the person. Default is None.

parameters.name: organization_name
parameters.description: Employer name to disambiguate a name match. Default is None.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Employer domain to disambiguate a name match. Default is None.

parameters.name: linkedin_url
parameters.description: LinkedIn profile URL of the person. Default is None.

parameters.name: reveal_work_email
parameters.description: When True, include the matched person's work email in the result. A successful match consumes an enrichment credit whether or not this is set, so the flag controls only whether the email value is surfaced. Default is False.

output.description: The matched person, or found=False when no match.

tools.name: GetApiUsage
tools.description:
Report per-endpoint rate limits and how many requests remain, so the agent
can pace a batch of lookups and avoid being throttled. Requires an Apollo
master API key.

output.description: Per-endpoint rate limits and remaining request counts.

tools.name: SearchOrganizations
tools.description:
Find companies in Apollo's database that match a firmographic profile so a
rep can prioritize the accounts worth working. Results include firmographics
inline unless include_firmographics is disabled. Company search consumes plan
credits; when the plan is out of credits the result reports
status=insufficient_credits with an empty list rather than failing.

parameters.name: keyword_tags
parameters.description: Industry or keyword tags to match, such as fintech or cybersecurity. Default is None.

parameters.name: locations
parameters.description: Headquarters locations, such as a city, state, or country. Default is None.

parameters.name: num_employees_ranges
parameters.description: Headcount ranges, each formatted as min,max (for example 51,200). Default is None.

parameters.name: revenue_min
parameters.description: Minimum annual revenue in whole dollars. Default is None.

parameters.name: revenue_max
parameters.description: Maximum annual revenue in whole dollars. Default is None.

parameters.name: technology_uids
parameters.description: Apollo technology UIDs the company must use. Default is None.

parameters.name: include_firmographics
parameters.description: When True, backfill each result with firmographics (industry, headcount, location, revenue, founded year) via enrichment so accounts can be ranked without a separate lookup; set False for a faster, lighter name/domain-only list. Default is True.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum companies to return, 1 to 100. Default is 25.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position into the matches. Default is 0 (first result).

tools.name: SearchPeople
tools.description:
Find people in Apollo's database by role and the firmographics of their
employer. Returns lightweight records without email or phone; use person
enrichment to reveal verified contact details.

parameters.name: person_titles
parameters.description: Job titles to match, such as VP of Sales. Matched loosely unless include_similar_titles is disabled. Default is None.

parameters.name: person_seniorities
parameters.description: Seniority levels of the person's current role. Default is None.

parameters.name: person_locations
parameters.description: Locations of the person, such as a city, state, or country. Default is None.

parameters.name: organization_locations
parameters.description: Locations of the person's employer headquarters. Default is None.

parameters.name: organization_num_employees_ranges
parameters.description: Employer headcount ranges, each formatted as min,max (for example 51,200). Default is None.

parameters.name: employer_domains
parameters.description: Company domains the person must work at. Default is None.

parameters.name: keywords
parameters.description: Free-text keywords matched across the person's profile. Default is None.

parameters.name: include_similar_titles
parameters.description: When True, titles match loosely (similar titles included). Set False to keep only people whose title exactly matches one of person_titles. Default is True.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum people to return, 1 to 100. Default is 25.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position into the matches. Default is 0 (first result).

keywords.value: SaaS B2B enterprise software revenue growth

summary:
Apollo toolkit integrates Apollo.io's sales intelligence platform with Arcade, enabling LLMs to search, enrich, and analyze companies and people programmatically.

## Capabilities

- **Company intelligence:** Search Apollo's database by firmographic filters (industry, size, revenue, location) and enrich a domain into a full account profile including funding and headcount.
- **People intelligence:** Search contacts by role and employer firmographics, then enrich individual profiles with current title and verified contact details using any available identifier (Apollo ID, name, email, or LinkedIn URL).
- **Credit-aware enrichment:** Both enrichment tools consume one credit per match and return `status=insufficient_credits` (rather than throwing an error) when the plan quota is exhausted, allowing agents to handle credit limits gracefully.
- **API rate-limit introspection:** Retrieve per-endpoint rate limits and remaining request counts so agents can pace batch workflows and avoid throttling.

## Secrets

- **`APOLLO_API_KEY`** — An Apollo.io API key that authenticates all requests. Obtain it from the Apollo dashboard under **Settings → Integrations → API Keys** ([Apollo API key docs](https://apolloio.github.io/apollo-api-docs/)). Note that `Apollo.GetApiUsage` specifically requires a **master API key** (not a limited/scoped key); for all other tools a standard API key is sufficient. Ensure the key belongs to an account with an active Apollo plan that has enrichment and search credits available.

See [Arcade secrets configuration](https://docs.arcade.dev/en/guides/create-tools/tool-basics/create-tool-secrets) for how to register secrets, or manage them directly at [https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets](https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets).
